name: Texas State Space Lab (Space Lab)
relevance: The Space Lab is a student-run research facility dedicated to developing space technology, small satellites (CubeSats), and ground-station operations.

name: Society for Space Exploration (SSE)
relevance: SSE is a research-driven student organization (SEDS Texas State chapter) that focuses on space technology, balloon missions, and small satellite development.

name: Bobcat Aerospace
relevance: Bobcat Aerospace is a student-led organization at Texas State focused on high-powered rocketry, propulsion systems, and aerospace engineering projects.

name: Astronomy Club
relevance: The Astronomy Club is dedicated to exploring the night sky, educational outreach, and operating the campus observatory for public viewing.

name: Robotics & Control (ROBOCO) Lab
relevance: The ROBOCO Lab conducts research on sensor fusion, artificial intelligence, and control systems for robotics and intelligent transportation.

name: Texas State Observatory
relevance: Located atop the Supple Science Building, the observatory is a research and public education facility equipped with a 16-inch telescope.

name: NASA STEM Educator Professional Development Collaborative (EPDC)
relevance: This NASA-funded collaborative at Texas State provides professional development for educators to engage students in NASA's missions and STEM.

name: X-Bow Systems Cooperative Research Partnership
relevance: A cooperative research agreement between Texas State and X-Bow Systems to test and validate advanced solid rocket motor technology.

name: Texas State Mars Drillers
relevance: The Mars Drillers is a manufacturing engineering team that competes in the NASA Texas Space Grant Consortium Design Challenge, developing robotic drills for planetary exploration.

name: Space Workforce Incubator for Texas (SWIFT)
relevance: SWIFT is an initiative aimed at preparing the Texas workforce for the booming aerospace and space exploration industry.

name: Society of Physics Students (SPS) - Texas State Chapter
relevance: The Texas State chapter of SPS regularly hosts events and speakers from organizations like NASA, focusing on astrophysics and professional development in physics.

name: IEEE Texas State - Robotics and Automation Society (RAS)
relevance: The IEEE branch at Texas State includes a focus on robotics and automation, often utilizing the makerspace for technical projects.

name: Advanced Composites Laboratory
relevance: This lab conducts research on composite materials used in aerospace applications and has strong leadership ties to the Bobcat Aerospace rocketry club.

name: Icarus Project
relevance: The Icarus project is a mission within the Space Lab and SSE to develop and launch balloon-borne experiments and small satellites.

name: Rapid Product and Process Development (RPD) Center
relevance: The RPD Center houses various NASA-funded projects and teams, including the Mars Drillers and robotic exploration initiatives.

name: NASA Texas Space Grant Consortium (TSGC) - Texas State Delegation
relevance: As a member institution of the TSGC, Texas State students and faculty participate in NASA-funded design challenges, scholarships, and research grants.

name: ASME Texas State Chapter
relevance: The ASME chapter at Texas State frequently participates in mechanical design and robotics projects, including those related to aerospace technology.

name: AFROTC Detachment 840 (Aerospace Studies)
relevance: The Department of Aerospace Studies (AFROTC) trains future Air Force and Space Force officers, offering specialized courses and leadership training in aerospace topics.
